name: Shipping / Tanker Rates
summary: Day 67 Tuesday. **KINETIC PAUSE BROKEN MAY 4 — IRAN ATTACKS US NAVY + UAE FUJAIRAH + SK HMM NAMU IN HORMUZ; US APACHE+SEAHAWK SINK 6 IRANIAN FAST BOATS UNDER PROJECT FREEDOM; 2 US DESTROYERS TRANSITED HORMUZ SUCCESSFULLY.** Frontline $37.06 (+0.1% Tue intraday, +1.3% 1M, +36.6% 3M); BOAT $41.52 (+0.3% Tue, +1.0% 1M, +18.7% 3M). VLCC TD3C MEG-China sustained ~$420-474K/day per Baltic Exchange (well above $250K-350K mid-March peak average). HMM Namu (180m Panama-flagged, SK-operated, 24 crew) damaged by explosion+fire near UAE inside Hormuz May 4 — FIRST commercial-fleet damage to South Korean shipping during crisis. Project Freedom: US-led task force convoying ships through Hormuz; 2 US destroyers transited successfully Day 1; 6 Iranian fast boats sunk by US helicopters in attempted attacks. Trump April 23 'destroy-mine-laying-boats' ROE in effect — May 4 was first known activation. AWRP at 0.8-1.5% (off March peaks) — re-spike risk on Project Freedom. Per-VLCC incremental cost stack ~$6-10M/transit. The transit is now ARMED-ESCORTED CONTESTED rather than de facto blocked. Houthis Day 73-74 still quiet. The contested-blockade-under-armed-escort regime is the new operational baseline.
